message:
fix: remove abortAllFetches on nav — caused double-click sidebar bug (#7771)

* fix: remove abortAllFetches on route change — caused double-click navigation

The abort cancelled in-flight fetches which threw AbortErrors that
interfered with React Router navigation, requiring a second click.
The concurrency reduction (8→4) from the same PR still helps with
connection pool headroom.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: watchdog loading page layout shift from tip appearing

The tip section faded in after 0.8s and the elapsed timer started
empty, both causing layout shifts. Now the tip is always visible
and the elapsed div reserves min-height so the page doesn't jump.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2,7 +2,6 @@ import { ReactNode, Suspense, lazy, useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react'
 import { useTranslation } from 'react-i18next'
 import { Link, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'
 import { Box, Wifi, WifiOff, X, Settings, Rocket, RotateCcw, Check, Loader2, RefreshCw, Plug } from 'lucide-react'
-import { abortAllFetches } from '../../hooks/useCachedData'
 import { Button } from '../ui/Button'
 import { Navbar } from './navbar/index'
 import { Sidebar } from './Sidebar'
@@ -63,9 +62,6 @@ function NavigationProgress() {
 
   useEffect(() => {
     if (location.pathname !== prevPath.current) {
-      // Abort all in-flight cluster fetches so browser connections are
-      // freed immediately for the new page's lazy chunk and data.
-      abortAllFetches()
       setIsNavigating(true)
       prevPath.current = location.pathname
       const timer = setTimeout(() => setIsNavigating(false), CLOSE_ANIMATION_MS)
